name: Canvas LMS API
what_it_does: REST API for Canvas LMS, the widely-used open-source learning management system, providing access to courses, assignments, grades, submissions, enrollments, and discussion boards.
use_cases: Automating assignment creation and grade entry for instructors
Building course analytics dashboards for student progress tracking
Syncing enrollment data with institutional student information systems
Extracting submission data for automated grading or plagiarism checking
Creating course content programmatically from external content sources
not_for: Non-Canvas LMS platforms (Moodle, Blackboard have separate APIs)
Real-time video conferencing integration (use Zoom/Meet APIs)
Consumer-facing e-learning outside institutional Canvas deployments
best_when: Your institution uses Canvas and you need to automate course administration, grading workflows, or build integrations with campus systems.
avoid_when: You're not using Canvas LMS or need a cloud-native LMS without self-hosting.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for user-context actions, API tokens (personal access tokens) for admin/automated tasks. Tokens are per-user and scoped. LTI integration available for embedded tools.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free and open-source for self-hosting. Canvas Cloud pricing for hosted instances.
pricing.paid_tiers: Canvas Cloud pricing for institutional hosted deployments
pricing.notes: Open-source and free to self-host. Instructure offers paid hosting (Canvas Cloud) for institutions. Free tier available via Instructure's free account at instructure.com.

performance.rate_limits: No published rate limits; practical limit ~3000 req/hour

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include status codes and human-readable messages but lack error codes. Validation errors can be verbose JSON with field-level details.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Agents must check for existing resources before creating to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Pagination uses Link headers (RFC 5988) not JSON body — agents must parse response headers
Rate limiting is enforced but not documented; Canvas may return 403 throttle errors
Per-instance URLs differ (school.instructure.com vs hosted.canvaslms.com) — no single global endpoint
GraphQL API is newer but less documented than REST; some features only in REST
FERPA compliance requires careful handling of student data — avoid logging personally identifiable info
API versions are not dated; some features differ between self-hosted and cloud versions

security_score_components.security_notes: LMS platform (Instructure). OAuth2 + developer key. Student data is FERPA-protected in US context. Admin tokens have broad access — use user tokens where possible.
